what_it_does: Vitest test suite for the backfill driver in the recollection package. It builds temporary project directories, writes synthetic Claude-Code-shaped JSONL transcript files, and asserts that backfillConversations captures conversations correctly, handles sidechains, resumes from appended lines, respects a max-conversations cap, remains idempotent, aligns with the incremental indexUnprocessed path, and tolerates per-file parse failures.
observations: Uses isolated TEST_* environment variables and a temp directory for each test.
Provides helper functions makeExchangeLines, countExchanges, and makeProject.
Suppresses console output during tests via suppressConsole.
Generates deterministic, unique text per (seq, sessionId) so each synthetic exchange would embed to a distinct vector.
Tests sidechain files named agent-*.jsonl are swept and stored.
Tests idempotency by running backfill twice over the same corpus and expecting zero new rows.
Tests resume by appending lines to an existing transcript and expecting only the new exchanges to be captured.
Tests a --max-convs cap and a follow-up uncapped run that picks up the remainder.
Includes a cross-path idempotency test between backfillConversations and indexUnprocessed to verify identical archive-path conventions.
Tests per-file failure tolerance with a deliberately malformed JSONL line.
standouts: The cross-path idempotency test explicitly guards against double-insertion between the backfill and incremental indexer paths.
The sidechain-capture test makes agent-*.jsonl inclusion a load-bearing requirement.
The resume test simulates a multi-hour run that stopped mid-file and later sees appended lines.
The max-convs test verifies bounded runs plus clean resume.
conventions: Uses TEST_* prefixed environment variables to avoid touching live store paths.
Cleans up temp directories and env vars in afterEach.
Asserts database state directly with better-sqlite3 in addition to function return values.
Comments explain the intent behind each behavioral assertion.
concerns: makeProject uses mkdirSync(join(dir, file, '..')) which works but is an unusual way to create the parent directory.
The broken.jsonl test asserts parser tolerance but does not verify filesFailed/filesSkipped accounting.
indexUnprocessed(1, true) is called with magic positional arguments whose meaning is not explained in this file.
The exact archive-path convention and HWM storage mechanism are assumed but not shown here.
notes: A behavioral-contract test suite that treats backfill idempotency, resume, sidechain coverage, and indexer alignment as first-class invariants.
questions: What do the arguments 1 and true mean in indexUnprocessed(1, true)?
How is the high-water mark (HWM) persisted and keyed per file?
What archive-path convention is shared between backfillConversations and indexUnprocessed?
How does the system distinguish a sidechain file from a main session file beyond the agent-*.jsonl name?
What embedding model and vector store are exercised by these synthetic exchanges?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it tests the backfill driver's core behaviors.
apparent_themes: Cross-path consistency between backfill and live indexer
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that backfillConversations is a safe, repeatable, bounded, and complete ingestion path that produces the same exchange identities as the live incremental indexer, even as the corpus grows or contains malformed files.
novelty: Synthetic Claude-Code JSONL generation with per-exchange unique content to guarantee distinct embeddings, plus an explicit cross-path idempotency contract between the bulk backfill and incremental indexing paths.
contribution: A set of regression tests and behavioral contracts for the backfill driver, covering full corpus capture, sidechain sweeping, idempotency, resume, bounded runs, cross-path identity alignment, and failure tolerance.
summary_for_embedding: A vitest suite that exercises the recollection backfill driver with synthetic Claude-Code JSONL transcripts to verify idempotency, resume, sidechain capture, bounded runs, and alignment with the incremental indexer.
intention_for_embedding: To establish and guard the behavioral contracts that make backfillConversations safe to run repeatedly over a growing conversation corpus.

symbols.does: Builds one user/assistant exchange pair as two JSONL lines with deterministic, unique content for a given sequence number and session id.
symbols.description: This helper constructs a user message line and an assistant message line in the Claude-Code JSONL shape, assigning stable uuids, timestamps, parent references, and content that includes the sequence and session id so each synthetic exchange is distinct.

symbols.does: Opens the temporary SQLite database and returns the total number of rows in the exchanges table.
symbols.description: A small assertion helper that creates a new better-sqlite3 connection to the test database, runs SELECT COUNT(*) FROM exchanges, closes the connection, and returns the count so tests can verify actual persisted state.

symbols.does: Creates a mock project directory under TEST_PROJECTS_DIR and writes the supplied file contents into it.
symbols.description: This helper sets up a project folder with arbitrary transcript files, creating parent directories as needed, so tests can construct multi-project corpora with main sessions and sidechain files.
